title: Swift Package Manager
content:
### Swift Package Manager (SwiftPM)

Swift Package Manager is Apple's official build system and dependency manager. It is integrated into the Swift toolchain and Xcode, making it easy to create, distribute, and consume Swift libraries and executables.

**Creating a Package:**

```bash
# Create a new library package
swift package init --name MyLibrary --type library

# Create an executable package
swift package init --name MyTool --type executable
```

**Package Structure:**

```
MyPackage/
  |-- Package.swift
  |-- Sources/
  |   |-- MyPackage/
  |       |-- MyFile.swift
  |-- Tests/
      |-- MyPackageTests/
          |-- MyFileTests.swift
```

**Package.swift (Manifest):**

```swift
// swift-tools-version: 5.9

import PackageDescription

let package = Package(
    name: "MyLibrary",
    platforms: [
        .iOS(.v16),
        .macOS(.v13)
    ],
    products: [
        .library(
            name: "MyLibrary",
            targets: ["MyLibrary"]
        ),
    ],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "https://github.com/example/Package.git", from: "1.0.0"),
        .package(path: "../LocalPackage"),
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(
            name: "MyLibrary",
            dependencies: [
                .product(name: "SomeModule", package: "Package"),
            ]
        ),
        .testTarget(
            name: "MyLibraryTests",
            dependencies: ["MyLibrary"]
        ),
    ]
)
```

**Manifest Sections:**

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `name` | Package name |
| `platforms` | Minimum platform versions |
| `products` | What the package produces (library, executable) |
| `dependencies` | External package dependencies |
| `targets` | Build targets with sources and dependencies |

**Common Commands:**

```bash
swift build           # Build the package
swift test            # Run tests
swift run             # Run executable
swift package update  # Update dependencies
swift package generate-xcodeproj  # Generate Xcode project
```

**Version Specification:**

```swift
.package(url: "...", from: "1.2.0")           // >=1.2.0, <2.0.0
.package(url: "...", exact: "1.2.3")          // Exactly 1.2.3
.package(url: "...", "1.2.0"..<"2.0.0")      // Range
.package(url: "...", branch: "main")          // Branch
.package(url: "...", revision: "abc123")      // Specific commit
```

**Resource Bundles:**

```swift
.target(
    name: "MyLibrary",
    resources: [
        .process("Resources"),  // Process images, JSON
        .copy("Data"),          // Copy as-is
    ]
)
```

> **Key Point:** SwiftPM is integrated into Xcode 11+. Most iOS projects use SwiftPM for dependency management instead of CocoaPods or Carthage.

SwiftPM supports resources, localization, C interoperability, and conditional compilation per platform.
